data.title: French OTC Base Load Electricity Price Surges 87.1%
data.description: The French over-the-counter (OTC) base load electricity price recently experienced a significant increase, rising by 87.1% to reach 112.25 euros per megawatt-hour, according to data from LSEG. This surge highlights the volatility in the energy market, which has been influenced by various factors including supply constraints and rising demand. Market participants are closely monitoring these developments as they assess the potential impact on energy costs and broader economic conditions.

data.title: Tazapay Secures $36 Million in Series B Extension to Enhance Global Operations
data.description: Tazapay has successfully closed its Series B extension round, raising a total of $36 million. According to NS3.AI, the company plans to utilize the newly acquired funds to expand its global licensing capabilities, develop agentic payment infrastructure, and bolster its market presence internationally. This strategic move aims to enhance Tazapay's operational efficiency and broaden its reach in the global market.

data.title: Denmark's Economy Faces Risks Amid Prolonged Middle East Conflict
data.description: Denmark's central bank has issued a warning regarding potential economic repercussions if the ongoing conflict in the Middle East continues. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns that prolonged instability in the region could adversely affect Denmark's economic performance. The central bank emphasized that the situation could lead to increased uncertainty and volatility in global markets, which may impact Denmark's trade and financial sectors. The warning comes as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East show no signs of abating, raising concerns about potential disruptions to international trade routes and energy supplies. Denmark's central bank is closely monitoring the situation and advises businesses and investors to remain vigilant in the face of these challenges.

data.title: South Korea's Vice Finance Minister Calls for Exporters' Cooperation to Stabilize Forex Market
data.description: South Korea's Vice Finance Minister has urged export companies to collaborate in maintaining stability in the foreign exchange market. According to Jin10, the call comes amid ongoing concerns about market volatility and its potential impact on the country's economy. The Vice Minister emphasized the importance of exporters' roles in ensuring a balanced and stable forex environment, which is crucial for economic growth and resilience. The government is closely monitoring the situation and is prepared to implement measures to support market stability if necessary.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Pony AI Aims for Global Expansion to Enhance Sales and Reduce Losses
data.description: China's Pony AI is actively pursuing international growth to increase sales and mitigate financial losses. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's strategic efforts to broaden its market presence beyond domestic borders. Pony AI, known for its advancements in autonomous driving technology, is focusing on expanding its operations globally to tap into new markets and opportunities. This move comes as the company seeks to strengthen its financial position and enhance its competitive edge in the rapidly evolving AI industry. The expansion strategy is part of Pony AI's broader plan to leverage its technological capabilities and drive revenue growth amid increasing competition in the autonomous vehicle sector.

data.title: Israeli officials claim to have killed the commander of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps Navy.
data.description: According to Israeli media reports, an Israeli official stated that Alireza Tangsiri, commander of the Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps Navy, was killed in an attack in the port of Bandar Abbas. Neither the Iranian nor the Israeli military has commented on the attack. Israeli media pointed out that Alireza Tangsiri was responsible for the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. (Jinshi)

data.title: Binance Alpha Launches TRADOOR Airdrop Round 2 With 242-Point Threshold and Dynamic Access Rules
data.description: Binance has launched the second wave of its Tradoor (TRADOOR) airdrop through the Binance Alpha program, introducing a dynamic participation model tied to user points.To qualify, users must hold at least 242 Alpha points, with each successful claim distributing 14 TRADOOR tokens on a first-come, first-served basis. Claiming the airdrop will consume 15 Alpha points per user.The event includes a flexible access mechanism. If the reward pool is not fully distributed, the eligibility threshold will decrease by 5 points every five minutes, potentially allowing broader participation over time.Users are required to complete confirmation on the event page within 24 hours of initiating the claim. Failure to do so will result in forfeiture of the allocated tokens.

data.title: Carta Expands Operations with New Hong Kong Office to Modernize Private Market Ecosystem
data.description: Global fintech company Carta, headquartered in California, has announced the opening of a new office in Hong Kong. According to Ming Pao, this move aims to establish a dual-hub network in Asia, enhancing the modernization of Hong Kong's $240 billion private market ecosystem. Carta offers the world's first one-stop software solution specifically designed for the private market. In 2021, the company set up its regional headquarters in Singapore and has recently completed an upgrade and expansion. With the launch of the Hong Kong office, Carta plans to connect two of Asia's most dynamic capital centers, providing services to fund managers in the region.

data.title: Aluminum Premiums Rise in Japan for April-June Deliveries
data.description: Aluminum premiums for deliveries in Japan from April to June have been set at $350 to $353 per ton, marking an increase of 79% to 81% compared to the current quarter. According to Jin10, this adjustment reflects changes in market dynamics and supply chain factors impacting the aluminum industry. The rise in premiums is attributed to various economic and geopolitical influences affecting global commodity markets. Stakeholders in the industry are closely monitoring these developments as they could have significant implications for pricing and supply strategies in the coming months.

data.title: Next Anticipates £15 Million Impact from Conflict as UK Consumer Confidence Declines
data.description: Next, a prominent UK retailer, is forecasting a financial impact of £15 million due to ongoing geopolitical tensions. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this development comes amid a significant drop in consumer confidence in the UK, reaching its lowest point since Keir Starmer became the leader of the Labour Party. The decline in confidence is attributed to various economic pressures affecting British households. As the situation unfolds, businesses and consumers alike are bracing for potential economic repercussions.

data.title: Odaily Skills is now available for free, featuring structured output of curated information that saves you tokens.
data.description: Odaily Skills are now available for free on ClawHub. Current media skills primarily consist of information feed encapsulation, limiting information effectiveness and usability. Odaily's new Skills are not simply full content reposting; instead, they filter and structure information based on the main site's real traffic and user behavior. The core modules include: 1. Today's Must-Watch; 2. Encrypted Market Analysis; 3. Tomorrow's Key Events; 4. Predicting Market Anomalies + Smart Whale Closing Price Tracking; 5. Modular API calls designed to reduce noise-driven input, providing AI with more usable content for decision-making. Further features will be continuously iterated and upgraded.

data.title: Upbit will launch KAT (Korean won) against...
data.description: According to an official announcement, Upbit will launch the KAT (Korean Won) pair.

data.title: Next CEO: UK Sales Unaffected by Iran Conflict, Middle East Demand Under Pressure
data.description: Next CEO Simon Wolfson stated that since the outbreak of the Iran conflict, there has been no noticeable decline in UK sales. According to Jin10, Wolfson noted that business in the Middle East experienced a significant drop in the initial days of the conflict, and demand remains under pressure.

data.title: Azerbaijan Receives Russian Humanitarian Aid for Iran
data.description: A freight train carrying over 300 tons of Russian humanitarian aid has arrived in Azerbaijan, according to Azerbaijan's national news agency, Azertac. The shipment, primarily consisting of pharmaceuticals and medical supplies, is destined for Iran. According to Jin10, the supplies will continue their journey by road transport. Two weeks ago, under the directive of Russian President Vladimir Putin, more than 13 tons of medical supplies were airlifted from Russia to Azerbaijan and subsequently transported to Iran by truck, as reported by TASS.

data.title: French Economic Indicators Decline Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: French industry and consumer confidence have seen a decline, with households expressing increased concerns about inflation following the outbreak of the Iran war. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of geopolitical tensions on economic sentiment in France. The conflict has led to uncertainty, affecting both industrial output and consumer spending. As inflation expectations rise, households are becoming more cautious, potentially influencing future economic activity. The situation underscores the interconnectedness of global events and their influence on national economies.

data.title: India Seeks Assistance to Mitigate LPG Shortage Impact on Steel Industry
data.description: India's Ministry of Steel has approached the Ministry of Petroleum for assistance to ensure that steel plants are not adversely affected by a shortage of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG), according to sources. India, the world's second-largest producer of crude steel, is currently facing its most severe LPG supply crisis in decades due to the conflict in Iran. According to Jin10, a source stated, "We have communicated with the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas to explore the most effective measures under the current conditions. Discussions are ongoing." Earlier reports indicated that small steel producers in India have warned of potential production halts due to the gas shortage.

data.title: Binance Alpha: The threshold for claiming the TRADOR airdrop is 242 points, and claiming it will consume 15 points.
data.description: Binance Alpha's second Tradoor (TRADOOR) airdrop has begun. Users need to hold at least 242 Alpha Points to participate and will receive 14 TRADOOR tokens on a first-come, first-served basis. If the reward pool is not fully allocated, the points threshold will decrease by 5 points every 5 minutes. Claiming the airdrop will consume 15 Alpha Points, and users must confirm on the event page within 24 hours; otherwise, they will be considered to have forfeited their claim.

data.title: Currys CEO Alex Baldock to Step Down After Eight Years
data.description: Currys CEO Alex Baldock is set to resign after leading the electricals retailer for eight years. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Baldock's role in revitalizing the company during his tenure. His departure marks the end of a significant era for Currys, as he played a crucial part in the company's turnaround. The search for his successor is underway, with the company aiming to maintain its positive trajectory.

data.title: France's March INSEE Business Confidence Index Matches Previous Value
data.description: France's March INSEE Business Confidence Index remained steady at 97, aligning with the previous month's figure, according to Jin10. The index slightly exceeded expectations, which were set at 96, indicating a stable business sentiment in the country. The consistency in the index suggests that French businesses are maintaining confidence despite broader economic uncertainties.

data.title: U.S. Court Dismisses Legal Protection Suit for Crypto Tool Pharos
data.description: A U.S. court has dismissed a lawsuit filed by Michael Lewellen seeking legal protection for Pharos, a non-custodial cryptocurrency tool. According to NS3.AI, the Northern District of Texas ruled that Lewellen did not demonstrate a substantial threat of prosecution, a necessary condition for a pre-enforcement challenge. The court also cited a Department of Justice memo advising prosecutors against pursuing crypto services for end-user actions or unintentional violations. The dismissal was issued without prejudice, allowing Lewellen the option to refile if the risk of enforcement becomes more imminent.
